title: [FIRRTL][LowerLayers] Fix instance input port capture
body:
When an instance input port is both captured in a layerblock and
connected to outside the layerblock, the LowerLayers pass was
incorrectly performing RAUW on the instance port, replacing it with
a node. This caused flow verification errors because instance input
ports have special flow semantics (sink flow but can be read from),
while nodes have source flow.

This commit fixes the issue by recursing on the driver of instance
input ports instead of creating a node for the port itself. When
the driver can support an inner symbol (e.g., a module port), the
XMR references it directly. When the driver cannot support an inner
symbol (e.g., an invalidvalue operation), a node is created from
the driver and the XMR references that node. This approach avoids
creating unnecessary intermediate nodes while maintaining correct
flow semantics.

The fix includes a comprehensive test case that validates both
scenarios: instance input ports driven by operations that cannot
support inner symbols, and those driven by module ports that can.

AI-assisted-by: Augment (Claude Sonnet 4.5)
